data.title: Designer Brands Extends and Refines Revolving Credit Facilities
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Designer Brands ( (DBI) ) has issued an update.

On February 27, 2026, Designer Brands Inc. amended its asset-based revolving credit agreement, slightly reducing the maximum commitment of its first-in-last-out term loan facility from $30 million to $29.5 million while maintaining the $600 million asset-based revolving facility. The amendment also extends the maturity of the credit facilities from March 30, 2027 to as late as February 27, 2031, aligns maturity with the company’s senior secured term loan if earlier, removes the term SOFR credit spread adjustment, introduces customary fees, and commits the company to specific timelines for transitioning its payment processing service provider, collectively refining liquidity terms and operational obligations for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (DBI) stock is a Hold with a $7.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Designer Brands stock, see the DBI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DBI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DBI is a Neutral.

DBI scores low-to-mid primarily due to weak financial fundamentals (declining revenue, negative profitability, and high leverage), only partly offset by improving gross margin and strong free-cash-flow improvement. Technical signals are neutral/mixed with limited momentum, while valuation is tempered by a negative P/E despite a moderate dividend. The earnings call was a relative bright spot with sequential improvement and debt reduction, but guidance still implies continued sales contraction.

To see Spark’s full report on DBI stock, click here.

More about Designer Brands

Designer Brands Inc. operates in the retail footwear and accessories industry, with operations in both the U.S. and Canada through various borrowing and guarantor subsidiaries. The company utilizes asset-based and first-in-last-out credit facilities to support its working capital needs and broader financing structure, reflecting a capital-intensive retail model that relies on revolving credit arrangements.

Average Trading Volume: 1,203,186

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $345.5M

data.title: Franklin Street Properties Secures New $320 Million Credit Facility
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Franklin Street Properties ( (FSP) ) has issued an update.

On February 26, 2026, Franklin Street Properties Corp. entered into a new secured credit agreement providing a $320 million credit facility, comprising $275 million of initial term loans and up to $45 million of delayed draw term loans maturing in 2029. The facility is secured by first-priority liens on substantially all company assets, carries a 9.0% initial interest rate, includes an exit fee on repayment, and imposes financial covenants such as minimum tangible net worth and liquidity thresholds.

The company used $258.5 million of initial borrowings, net of original issue discount, to refinance and retire approximately $249 million of existing indebtedness under prior bank credit lines and a note purchase agreement on February 26, 2026. Management said the refinancing removes near‑term debt maturity pressure, avoids forced asset sales, and funds tenant and building improvements, thereby providing flexibility to execute property‑level initiatives and continue a broad review of strategic alternatives aimed at maximizing shareholder value in a challenging office market.

The most recent analyst rating on (FSP) stock is a Hold with a $0.81 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Franklin Street Properties stock, see the FSP Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FSP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FSP is a Neutral.

The overall stock score is primarily driven by weak financial performance and bearish technical indicators. The company’s financial challenges, including declining revenues and profitability, are significant risks. Technical analysis further indicates negative momentum, with the stock trading below key moving averages and in oversold conditions. Valuation concerns are underscored by a negative P/E ratio, despite a high dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on FSP stock, click here.

More about Franklin Street Properties

Franklin Street Properties Corp., based in Wakefield, Massachusetts, is a Maryland corporation that operates as a real estate investment trust focused on institutional-quality office properties in the U.S. The company targets value-oriented investments in urban infill and central business district assets, emphasizing core markets such as Dallas, Denver, Houston and Minneapolis for long-term growth, appreciation and current income.

FSP’s portfolio strategy centers on leasing and improving office assets in major U.S. metros, seeking to enhance property-level performance while navigating an uneven office market environment. The firm positions itself to balance income generation with capital appreciation, using financing structures and asset-level initiatives to preserve strategic flexibility and maximize shareholder value.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Quanta Services Adopts 2026 Performance-Based Incentive Framework
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Quanta Services ( (PWR) ).

On February 26, 2026, Quanta Services’ board Compensation Committee approved a 2026 incentive framework comprising an annual incentive plan for corporate staff, a long-term incentive plan for senior leadership and a discretionary plan for all employees. Under this structure, executive officers and other employees can receive cash, restricted stock units and performance stock units, with equity grants issued under the existing 2019 Omnibus Equity Incentive Plan or future board-approved plans.

The committee set 2026 annual performance metrics based on EBITDA, EBITDA margin and safety, while long-term incentives for the 2026–2028 period will hinge on return on invested capital, earnings per share and total stockholder return. The move reinforces a pay-for-performance culture that more tightly links employee and executive compensation to profitability, capital efficiency, safety outcomes and shareholder value, aligning management incentives with key financial and operational priorities.

The most recent analyst rating on (PWR) stock is a Buy with a $570.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Quanta Services stock, see the PWR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PWR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PWR is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (scaled revenue, improved leverage, solid cash generation) and a bullish earnings outlook supported by record backlog and robust free-cash-flow guidance. These positives are tempered by expensive valuation (very high P/E and minimal yield) and technically overbought signals that can increase near-term pullback risk.

To see Spark’s full report on PWR stock, click here.

More about Quanta Services

The company’s services support large-scale utility and energy customers, with a focus on long-term capital projects and maintenance programs tied to grid modernization and energy transition trends.

Diana Shipping ( (DSX) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 4, 2026, Diana Shipping Inc. announced that a wholly owned subsidiary signed a new time charter contract with SwissMarine Pte. Ltd. in Singapore for its Ice Class Panamax dry bulk vessel m/v Crystalia. The deal underscores the company’s strategy of securing employment for its fleet at attractive rates in a volatile dry bulk market.

The charter, expected to commence on March 11, 2026, sets a gross rate of $16,200 per day, minus a 5% commission, through at least March 10, 2027 and up to May 10, 2027. Replacing the vessel’s prior charter at $13,900 per day, the new contract is anticipated to generate roughly $5.78 million in gross revenue over the minimum period, enhancing earnings visibility for Diana Shipping’s 36-vessel, 4.1 million dwt fleet.

data.title: Smith Micro Raises Insider-Backed Capital Amid Leadership Transition
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Smith Micro Software ( (SMSI) ) has provided an update.

On March 4, 2026, Smith Micro Software agreed to raise approximately $4.9 million through a private placement of secured convertible notes and accompanying warrants to accredited investors, including its CEO’s family trust and its COO/CFO, with proceeds earmarked primarily to repay up to $2.2 million of notes maturing March 31, 2026 and for general corporate purposes. The new notes, bearing 8% interest and maturing in 2029, are secured by certain company assets, feature conversion and ownership limits, and are expected to close around March 6, 2026, moves that bolster liquidity but add dilution risk and underscore the firm’s reliance on insider financing.

The company also announced that, effective March 31, 2026, long-time leader William W. Smith Jr. will transition to Executive Chairman, with Timothy C. Huffmyer becoming President and CEO and Bethany M. Braund assuming the CFO and Treasurer roles, formalizing a leadership handover as Smith Micro seeks a path back to profitability. Separately, on March 4, 2026, Smith Micro reported fourth-quarter 2025 revenue of $4.0 million and a GAAP net loss of $4.7 million, and full-year 2025 revenue of $17.4 million with a GAAP net loss of $30.1 million, showing shrinking top-line but improved net loss versus 2024 and highlighting ongoing financial strain given year-end cash of just $1.5 million despite higher gross margins and reduced non-GAAP losses.

Management emphasized substantial cost reductions, a more focused investment strategy and a strengthened SafePath OS product lineup aimed at kids’ and seniors’ phones and expanded Tier 1 carrier opportunities. The combination of insider-funded convertible debt, leadership changes and mixed 2025 results signals an attempt to stabilize the balance sheet and extend the runway for a turnaround, while keeping investors’ attention on execution risk and the need to translate product momentum into sustainable growth and profitability.

The most recent analyst rating on (SMSI) stock is a Hold with a $0.56 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Smith Micro Software stock, see the SMSI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SMSI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SMSI is a Neutral.

SMSI scores low primarily due to weak financial performance—declining revenue, very large losses, and negative cash flow—reinforced by bearish technicals with the price below all major moving averages. Cost reductions and margin improvement plans from the earnings call provide some offset, but listing-risk headlines and reliance on incremental financing keep the overall score constrained.

To see Spark’s full report on SMSI stock, click here.

More about Smith Micro Software

Smith Micro Software, Inc. is a U.S.-based provider of mobile software that simplifies and enhances the mobile experience for leading wireless service providers worldwide. Its portfolio includes SafePath OS solutions focused on phones for children and seniors, family digital lifestyle tools and voice messaging capabilities, targeting engagement across smartphones and consumer IoT devices.

The company serves Tier 1 carriers and other operators seeking to deepen subscriber relationships and monetize value-added services. By focusing on high-margin software platforms and connected-lifestyle use cases, Smith Micro positions itself within the competitive telecom software and digital services market, where product breadth and integration with carrier ecosystems are key differentiators.

Average Trading Volume: 107,370

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $13.11M

data.title: Crane Harbor Issues 2025 PFIC Annual Tax Statement
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp. Class A ( (CHAC) ).

On March 4, 2026, Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp. released its PFIC Annual Statement for the 2025 tax year to holders of its Class A ordinary shares, covering the period from January 2, 2025 to December 31, 2025. The statement provides per‑share, per‑day ordinary earnings of $0.0011219650 with no net capital gains, enabling U.S. investors to evaluate whether to make a Qualified Electing Fund election for U.S. tax purposes.

The company reported that no cash or property was distributed or deemed distributed to shareholders during the 2025 taxable period and confirmed it will allow investors to inspect records needed to verify PFIC ordinary earnings and gains. The disclosure clarifies tax reporting data for shareholders and underscores that investors must consult their own advisers to address PFIC compliance and any additional U.S. filing obligations linked to their holdings in the SPAC.

More about Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp. Class A

Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) whose Class A ordinary shares trade under the ticker CHAC. As a blank-check company, it primarily holds passive financial assets while seeking a business combination, which can cause it to be treated as a passive foreign investment company (PFIC) for U.S. federal income tax purposes.

The entity’s structure and investment profile mean U.S. shareholders may face specialized tax reporting and elections, particularly under the PFIC and Qualified Electing Fund (QEF) regimes. Its activities are largely financial and transactional rather than operational, focusing on capital raising and potential mergers rather than producing goods or services.

On February 27, 2026, Jack in the Box stockholders ratified the Board’s Stockholder Protection Rights Agreement, originally adopted in July 2025 and amended in September 2025, extending its expiration to July 1, 2028, unless earlier terminated or superseded by certain corporate transactions. The continuation of this rights plan is aimed at safeguarding the company’s shareholder base and corporate control structure, reinforcing defenses against potential accumulation of significant stakes that could impact governance or strategic direction.

At the same annual meeting on February 27, 2026, shareholders elected all ten director nominees and approved all four other proposals, including ratification of KPMG LLP as auditor for fiscal 2026 and an advisory endorsement of executive compensation for fiscal 2025. data.title: Sirius XM Announces Tender Offer for 2026 Senior Notes
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Sirius XM Holdings ( (SIRI) ).

On March 4, 2026, Sirius XM Holdings announced the pricing terms for a previously launched cash tender offer by subsidiary Sirius XM Radio LLC to purchase any and all of its outstanding 3.125% Senior Notes due September 1, 2026, setting a purchase price of $994.64 per $1,000 principal amount plus accrued interest. The offer, scheduled to expire at 5:00 p.m. New York time on March 4, 2026, is not subject to a minimum tender condition and is expected to be funded with proceeds from a contemporaneous senior notes offering and cash on hand, potentially followed by redemption or satisfaction and discharge of any remaining notes, marking another step in the company’s ongoing balance sheet management.

Holders whose notes are accepted will receive accrued and unpaid interest to, but not including, the initial payment date expected on March 5, 2026, with guaranteed delivery settlements anticipated by March 9, 2026, subject to the offer’s conditions. The transaction framework gives SiriusXM flexibility to retire or defease its 2026 notes through the tender and, if needed, subsequent redemption actions, which could reduce near-term debt maturities and interest costs while reshaping its capital structure without obligating the company to purchase a minimum amount of notes.

The most recent analyst rating on (SIRI) stock is a Hold with a $23.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sirius XM Holdings stock, see the SIRI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SIRI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SIRI is a Neutral.

Sturm Ruger & Company ( (RGR) ) has provided an update.

Sturm, Ruger & Company reported that 2025 was a difficult year for the firearms and broader consumer durables industries, but the company managed modest top-line growth and market share gains, with estimated sell-through up 4.5% despite a 4.1% decline in adjusted NICS background checks. On March 2, 2026, executives detailed that full-year net sales rose 1.9% to $546 million while the company swung to a net loss of $0.27 per share due to nonrecurring costs tied to leadership transition, product rationalization and organizational realignment, though it remained debt-free, generated $54 million in operating cash flow, invested heavily in capacity and innovation, and returned $36 million to shareholders via dividends and buybacks.

Management highlighted that adjusted 2025 earnings were $0.84 per share, supported by strong demand for new launches, including 65 models and three new platforms such as the Glenfield by Ruger rifle and Red Label III shotgun. Looking ahead, the company outlined a 2026 plan centered on sustaining revenue in a flat-to-down market, expanding operating margins through structural efficiencies, accelerating innovation in both firearms and accessories, and increasing production capacity under its Ruger 2030 strategy, moves that signal an emphasis on long-term profitable growth and competitive positioning despite industry headwinds.

data.title: AmBase Secures New Litigation Funding Amid Liquidity Strain
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from AmBase ( (ABCP) ) is now available.

On March 2, 2026, AmBase entered into a new litigation funding agreement with Chairman and CEO Richard A. Bianco, under which he will provide up to $6 million, including converting $4 million of existing promissory notes, to finance the company’s operations and ongoing litigation over the 111 West 57th property, with detailed tiered participation for Bianco in any future litigation recoveries. Separately, BARC Investments exercised its option to convert a $2 million note issued in August 2024 into a pari passu litigation funding arrangement, further reducing AmBase’s share of any eventual litigation proceeds as the company continues to face going-concern uncertainties and explores additional financing and strategic alternatives to meet its capital needs.

The Bianco agreement immediately injects $1 million for litigation expenses and another $1 million for working capital and related costs while extending the maturity of accrued interest, thereby providing short-term liquidity but at the cost of granting Bianco a significant priority claim on future recoveries. The BARC conversion similarly preserves cash by deferring cash repayment in favor of sharing in potential litigation proceeds on the same terms as Bianco’s funding, underscoring that AmBase’s ability to continue operating and realize value from its 111 West 57th investment now depends heavily on securing further capital and achieving successful outcomes in its complex, long-running disputes.

The most recent analyst rating on (ABCP) stock is a Sell with a $0.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on AmBase stock, see the ABCP Stock Forecast page.

An update from Genmab ( (GMAB) ) is now available.

On March 4, 2026, Genmab A/S filed a Form 6-K in the United States, updating investors with its revised Articles of Association, which formally set out the company’s corporate structure and capital framework. The filing confirms a registered share capital of DKK 64,250,721 and designates English as the group language, underscoring Genmab’s international orientation.

The updated Articles provide the board of directors with broad authorizations, valid mainly until March 12, 2029, to increase share capital with or without pre-emptive rights, issue employee warrants and convertible debt, and adjust the Articles accordingly. These measures, which stem from shareholder decisions between 2021 and 2025, give Genmab significant flexibility to fund growth, incentivize staff and manage its capital structure, potentially affecting future dilution and governance for existing shareholders.

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Regional Health Properties ( (RHEP) ) is now available.

On February 27, 2026, Regional Health Properties, Inc. and Erin Property Holdings, LLC entered into forbearance agreements with Cadence Bank related to defaults under a $5 million USDA-backed note and an $800,000 SBA-backed note, both originally due in 2036. The borrower agreed to make a one-time forbearance payment and cover the 2026 USDA annual renewal fee by February 27, 2026, while the lender agreed to forbear from exercising remedies over the payment defaults until February 1, 2027.

During the forbearance period, the company and the borrower must continue making regular monthly principal and interest payments under the existing loan terms, with interest continuing to accrue. At the end of the forbearance period, all remaining balances on the USDA and SBA notes, including principal, interest, late charges, and statutory attorney’s fees, will become due, underscoring continued refinancing and liquidity risk for the company and its stakeholders.

Crown Holdings ( (CCK) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On February 26, 2026, Crown Holdings, Inc. announced that veteran packaging executive Michael P. Doss was elected to its Board of Directors, effective March 3, 2026, bringing the Board’s size to ten members. Doss, the former President, Chief Executive Officer and Director of Graphic Packaging Corporation from 2016 to 2025 and current Director of Regal Rexnord Corporation, is expected to join Crown’s Audit Committee and will receive standard non-employee director compensation.

Crown Chairman and CEO Timothy J. Donahue highlighted that Doss adds substantial senior leadership experience in the packaging industry, as well as deep knowledge of manufacturing, commercial operations, international business and public company governance. The appointment signals Crown’s effort to strengthen board-level expertise in its core industry and operational disciplines, with no special arrangements or related-party transactions involved in Doss’s selection, underscoring standard governance practices for stakeholders.
